rulings.Case Title: DAVID BECK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS(B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 15, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Monterey Mechanical Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claim. Even if they are admissible, the declarations of Ms. Rentner-Glen and Mr. Hamilton merely create a triable issue whether defendant was present at the two identified sites of alleged exposure when read in conjunction with Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Red Top Electric Co. Emeryville, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied because defendant's separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Even if the court considered defendant's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action, it would be denied for the same reasons summary judgment is denied. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Anderson, Rowe & Buckley, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the fireproofing to which defendant's employees exposed Mr. McClain contained asbestos. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied because defendant's separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Even if the court considered defendant's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action, it would be denied for the same reasons summary judgment is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Todd Shipyards Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to the causation issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Hixson's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to defendant's argument based on Privette v. Superior Court and its progeny, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that even if plaintiffs cannot demonstrate that an exception to Privette applies, plaintiffs' claim still fail. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant L.J. Kruse Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 5 and 6 (premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages) and off calendar as to issues 1 through 4 (negligence and products liability). As to summary judgment, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation and fireproofing) attributable to defendant. (Plaintiffs' separate statement, facts nos. 1-7.) Summary adjudication is denied as to the premises owner/contractor liability cause of action because defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that even if plaintiffs cannot demonstrate that defendant retained control of decedent's jobsites, plaintiffs cause of action still fails. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's failure to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim for punitive damages precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's separate statement contains no allegedly undisputed facts regarding punitive damages. Plaintiffs have dismissed their causes of action for negligence (survival and wrongful death) and products liability (survival and wrongful death). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Cantley's declaration creates a triable issue whether the sophisticated user defense applies. (Plaintiffs' response to defendant's Separate Statement, facts nos. 23-24; plaintiffs' Separate Statement, facts nos. 10-17.) Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied because defendant's separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Even if the court considered defendant's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action, it would be denied for the same reasons summary judgment is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied because defendant's notice and separate statement failed to separately state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Even if the court considered defendant's motion for summary adjudication, it would be denied for the same reasons summary judgment is denied. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation cause of action and punitive damages claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220.
